title: Panel 4: “The Challenge of Famine and Pandemics — The Coincidence of Opposites or Mass Extinction?”
description:
Moderator: Dennis Speed, Schiller Institute

2:02 Dr. Joycelyn Elders, former Surgeon General of the United States, “A Global Health Mission for the Youth”
12:05 Marcia Merry Baker, Co-Editor, Executive Intelligence Review, “The World Famine and the Need to Double Agricultural Production”
33:14 Dr. Khadijah Lang, Chairman, National Medical Association (NMA) Council on International Affairs; President, Golden State Medical Association
41:56  Mike Callicrate (Colorado), Cattleman, Founder of Ranch Foods Direct, policy advocate, operator of Mike’s “No Bull” Blog
51:32 Dr. Shirley Evers-Manley, Interim Dean, Alcorn University School of Nursing, Mississippi, USA; Chairman, Global Health Committee, National Black Nurses Association
1:10:12 Bob Baker (USA), Schiller Institute Agricultural Liaison, “Stand Together for Food, Health and the Future.”
1:15:22 Bill Bullard, (Montana), CEO of R-CALF USA, (Ranchers-Cattlemen Action Legal Fund United Stockgrowers of America
1:20:50 Dr. Walter Faggett – Former Chief Medical Officer, Washington D.C. Dept of Health, co-chair DC Ward 8 Health Council, “D.C. Project: Community Healthcare workers Indispensable Role in Defeating the Pandemic”
1:30:06 Nicole Phrang, Kansas Cattle Rancher, Secretary-Treasurer, Kansas Cattlemen’s Association
1:36:22 James Benham, Indiana Farmer, President of the Indiana Farmers Union; National Board Member of the National Farmers Union
1:45:47 Fred Wills


Question and Answer Session
